matches.snippet: ribbon development, from contact bridge to lease lapse, only two millium two humbered and eighty thausig nine humbered and sixty radiolumin lines to the wustworts of a Finntown's generous poet's office.
matches.reasoning: Direct reference to Finntown, invoking Finn in a place-name. Joyce places Finntown in Dublin topography, literalizing the Finn/Dublin geography conflation.

matches.matched_text: Rolf the Ganger, Rough the Gangster
matches.snippet: Hispano-Cathayan-Euxine, Castillian - Emeratic - Hebridian, Espanol - Cymric-Smithy no home, Helleniky. Rolf the Ganger, Rough the Gangster, not a feature alike and the face the same.
matches.reasoning: Rolf the Ganger (a Scandinavian/Viking figure) paired with Rough the Gangster in a list of historical/mythic avatars. Joyce conflates different culture-heroes and trickster figures; these may represent alternative avatars of the eternal return motif (like Finn/Finnegan).

matches.snippet: To house as wise fool ages builded. Sow byg eat.
matches.reasoning: Homophonic pun on 'big' — the sleeping giant HCE is the 'big' man/macintosh figure. 'Byg' echoes the giant motif while 'eat' puns on 'ate' (consumed/fallen).
